title: Fetch, Response Admission, Abort Signals, Time Budgets, and Error Causes · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A public science-learning team incrementally builds injected bounded abort-aware Fetch client from original fixed PokéAPI payloads while retaining prior TypeScript, HTTP, Git, repository-gate, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of injected bounded abort-aware Fetch client while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: Inject a narrow fetch-compatible port and keep Request, Response, and validation policy separate from domain commands.
Classify ok, not found, other 4xx, 5xx, redirects, media type, and unexpected response before parsing domain JSON.
Compose caller cancellation with bounded timeout using AbortSignal APIs and preserve the first meaningful reason.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 7758 during an out-of-order Promise fault rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate Fetch status, bytes, unknown JSON, composed abort, causes, and timer cleanup; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node or network effects; then change one command, token, EOF, abort, or output-width case and preserve the session model. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the workshop evidence note for Pokedex Ts Fetch Fault Tests: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 5779 during an offline mobile-library catalog migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate Fetch status, bytes, unknown JSON, composed abort, causes, and timer cleanup; unknown external values must not cross the runtime validator; then change one catch, owned record, export, location relation, or history transition without mutating prior state. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test exact URL, options, headers, status, media type, JSON, timeout, caller abort, network failure, oversized response, and cause mapping.
steps.why: Retrieving Pokedex Ts Fetch Fault Tests through the concrete injected bounded abort-aware Fetch client case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test exact URL, options, headers, status, media type, JSON, timeout, caller abort, network failure, oversized response, and cause mapping.

hints: Name the Pokedex Ts Fetch Fault Tests precondition or invariant that the workshop case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A workshop TypeScript team handles case 5779 during an offline mobile-library catalog migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate Fetch status, bytes, unknown JSON, composed abort, causes, and timer cleanup; unknown external values must not cross the runtime validator; then change one catch, owned record, export, location relation, or history transition without mutating prior state. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test exact URL, options, headers, status, media type, JSON, timeout, caller abort, network failure, oversized response, and cause mapping.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A mocked resolved JSON object proves Fetch semantics.

checks.description: The cumulative typescript portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: pokedex-ts-fetch-fault-tests-49uzne.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: pokedex-ts-fetch-fault-tests-49uzne(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?function\s+evidence_pokedex_ts_fetch_fault_tests_49uzne\s*\([^)]*\)\s*:\s*Readonly<\{[^}]+\}>\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?requests < 5)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?admitted < 1 \|\| rejected < 1)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?admitted \+ rejected !== requests)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?leakedTimers > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
